title: Emotional intelligence (Emotional Quotient, EQ)
summary: 9609 A Level — Goleman's EQ components, link to leadership effectiveness, and developing EQ.
learningObjectives: Explain link between EQ and leadership performance.
Evaluate EQ in case leadership scenarios.

simpleExplanation.title: Leading with emotional skill
simpleExplanation.summary: Emotional intelligence (EQ) is the ability to recognise, understand, and manage your own emotions and those of others. High-EQ leaders handle conflict, build trust, and motivate more effectively than IQ alone.
steps: Empathy — understand others' feelings and perspectives.
Social skills — communicate, influence, and resolve conflict.

flashcards.back: Ability to perceive, use, understand, and manage emotions effectively.
Knowing your triggers, strengths, and impact on others.
Staying calm, avoiding rash decisions when stressed.
Reading how others feel — core to people-oriented leadership.
Building rapport, persuading, managing teams and conflict.
IQ = cognitive ability; EQ = interpersonal effectiveness — both matter for leaders.
Public outbursts, ignores feedback, high turnover in team.
Yes — coaching, feedback, mindfulness; unlike fixed trait theory.

sections.content: **Emotional intelligence** (7.3.3) completes **Topic 7.3**. Link EQ to **7.2.4 barriers** (empathy overcomes status fear) and **2.2 motivation** (recognition needs).

sections.question: Division head publicly criticises project manager in team meeting over missed deadline; two team members resign next month citing 'toxic culture.' Analyse using EQ framework.
sections.solution:
**Self-awareness lacking:** Leader doesn't see **humiliation impact** on team psyche.

**Self-regulation failure:** **Public anger** instead of **private constructive** feedback.

**Empathy missing:** Ignores **reasons** for delay (supplier issue in case?) — jumps to blame.

**Social skills damaged:** **Trust collapsed** — remaining staff **disengage** (2.2.3 Mayo social needs).

**Fix:** **Apology**, **1:1 coaching** for leader, **feedback training** — develop EQ; **360° reviews** track behaviour change.
